2604.09418.title: Automated Instruction Revision (AIR): A Structured Comparison of Task Adaptation Strategies for LLM
2604.09418.paper_summary: This paper studies Automated Instruction Revision (AIR), a rule-induction-based method for adapting large language models (LLMs) to downstream tasks using limited task-specific examples. We position AIR within the broader landscape of adaptation strategies, including prompt optimization, retrieval-based methods, and fine-tuning. We then compare these approaches across a diverse benchmark suite designed to stress different task requirements, such as knowledge injection, structured extraction, label remapping, and logical reasoning. The paper argues that adaptation performance is strongly task-dependent: no single method dominates across all settings. Across five benchmarks, AIR was strongest or near-best on label-remapping classification, while KNN retrieval performed best on closed-book QA, and fine-tuning dominated structured extraction and event-order reasoning. AIR is most promising when task behavior can be captured by compact, interpretable instruction rules, while retrieval and fine-tuning remain stronger in tasks dominated by source-specific knowledge or dataset-specific annotation regularities.
2604.09418.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文研究了自動化指令修訂（AIR），這是一種基於規則歸納的方法，用於利用有限的任務特定範例將大型語言模型（LLMs）調整到下游任務。我們將AIR定位於更廣泛的適應策略範疇中，包括提示優化、基於檢索的方法和微調。接著，我們在一個多樣的基準套件中比較這些方法，該套件旨在強調不同的任務需求，例如知識注入、結構化提取、標籤重映射和邏輯推理。論文主張，適應性能與任務密切相關：沒有單一方法在所有設置中都佔優。 在五個基準中，AIR在標籤重映射分類上表現最強或接近最佳，而KNN檢索在閉卷問答中表現最佳，微調則在結構化提取和事件順序推理中佔主導地位。當任務行為可以通過簡潔、可解釋的指令規則捕捉時，AIR最具潛力，而在以源特定知識或數據集特定註釋規律為主的任務中，檢索和微調仍然更強。
2604.09418.author: Solomiia Bilyk et.al.
2604.09418.authors: Solomiia Bilyk, Volodymyr Getmanskyi, Taras Firman
